summarization.a: The spectra chart shows X-ray diffraction patterns of iron oxide films grown at 500 °C. Strong hematite (Fe₂O₃) peaks are observed, with additional weaker magnetite (Fe₃O₄) reflections, indicating mixed-phase crystallinity at higher growth temperature.
summarization.b: The spectra chart shows X-ray diffraction patterns of iron oxide films grown at 400 °C. Both hematite and magnetite phases are present, with magnetite peaks becoming more pronounced compared to the higher-temperature case.
summarization.c: The spectra chart shows X-ray diffraction patterns of iron oxide films grown at 375 °C. Magnetite-related peaks dominate the spectrum, while hematite reflections are weaker, indicating a phase shift toward magnetite at lower growth temperature.

c.question: Which iron oxide phases are identified in the spectra?

a.question: What quantity is plotted on the x-axis of these spectra?
Does magnetite become more prominent at lower growth temperatures?

b.question: How does the crystalline phase composition change with decreasing growth temperature?
b.answer: As the growth temperature decreases from 500 °C to 375 °C, the diffraction patterns show a shift from hematite-dominated peaks toward magnetite-dominated peaks. This shows a temperature-driven phase evolution in the iron oxide films.
